title: Agent operating systems are becoming the new app

whats_new.text: Three creators independently converged on the same blueprint: wrap Claude in a personal 'agent OS' — skills + connectors + persistent memory.
Claude's managed agents add credential vaults, session isolation, and a nightly memory 'dream' — the missing pieces for selling agents to clients.
Hermes, an always-on agent living on a $10 VPS, got setup tutorials from three different channels in one week.
Fable 5 is out — and leaves Claude subscription plans on June 22, moving to usage-based pricing.
Orchestration craft is maturing: six named workflow patterns and a subagent design checklist replaced vague 'use agents' advice.

act.text: Steal one agent-OS layer this week: start with Nate's skill-gated subagents — smallest step, biggest payoff.
Try Fable 5 on your current plan before the June 22 cutoff.
If you want an agent that works while your laptop sleeps, follow Eric's 15-minute Hermes VPS setup.
Borrow Matt Pocock's stateful-lesson pattern for anything you're learning right now.

clusters.headline: Three channels, one blueprint: the personal agent OS
clusters.why_it_matters: You're already building one — agr-informed is this pattern; these videos show the next layers.
about: Ben AI: 80% of the value is a personalized daily overview — start with an Obsidian overlay, not a web app.
Claude managed agents demoed: per-agent credential vaults, sessions, memory stores, nightly 'dream' consolidation.
Liam Ottley runs the identical stack (skills + Notion + Apify + Higgsfield) as a one-person agency.
helps: A proven architecture for turning Claude from chat into a compounding system — for yourself or clients.
start: Watch Ben's MVP segment (11:08) and sketch the 5 things you want to see each morning.
Open platform.claude.com and create one managed agent with a single skill.

clusters.headline: Hermes: the always-on agent that lives on a $10 VPS
clusters.why_it_matters: An agent that keeps working while your laptop is closed — and it just crossed into the mainstream.
about: One-click Docker deploy on a cheap VPS; you talk to it through Telegram.
Runs cron jobs and a kanban board that moves its own tickets to done.
helps: Eric's standout demo: a daily research funnel filtering 400 GitHub repos down to the 5 worth attention — your briefing pattern, for any firehose.
start: Follow Eric's VPS setup (10:06); Tim and Metics have beginner walkthroughs if you get stuck.

sources.title: Hermes Agent: My Top 3 Use Cases + Full VPS Setup
sources.note: Top 3 use cases + full Hostinger VPS setup

clusters.headline: How the top 1% actually orchestrate their agents
clusters.why_it_matters: The practical curriculum for the multi-agent patterns you use in Claude Code every day.
about: Eric names six reusable workflow patterns: fan-out, classify-and-act, adversarial verify, generate-and-filter, tournament, loop-until-done.
Honest costs: one comment-analysis run burned 2M tokens in 11 minutes — he caps the thinking ceiling.
helps: Nate's subagent checklist: precise YAML descriptions for self-triggering, read-only tools, turn limits, Haiku for cheap filtering.
start: Pick ONE pattern from Eric's list (05:01) and apply it to a task you repeat weekly.

sources.title: Claude UltraCode Works Better When You Do These 5 Things
sources.note: Six workflow patterns, with real token costs

sources.title: How to Build Claude Subagents Better Than 99% of People

clusters.headline: Fable 5 is here — and leaves subscriptions June 22
clusters.why_it_matters: Under two weeks to evaluate Fable 5 on the plan you already pay for.
about: Tiers clarified: Fable 5 is the general release; Mythos 5 is the cyber-hardened variant at ~2x Opus pricing.
Fable at low effort ≈ Opus 4.8 at max reasoning for quality per dollar.
helps: Nate's contrarian take: agentic loops mostly burn tokens on general knowledge work — save them for true iterative refinement.
start: Run one of your real daily tasks on Fable 5 this week and compare against your current model.

sources.title: Claude Mythos is Finally Here.
sources.note: Fable vs Mythos tiers and the June 22 cutoff

clusters.headline: Make the AI your tutor, not just your intern
clusters.why_it_matters: Direct validation for your /teach feature — with two patterns to steal.
about: Matt Pocock's stateful /teach skill: a 'mission' file on why you're learning, interactive HTML lessons, a learning record that adapts.
Jeff Su's rule: 'the right context will always beat the perfect prompt.'
helps: Turns any topic from your briefing into a structured course that remembers where you left off.
start: Watch Pocock's zone-of-proximal-development demo (07:06), then try /teach on today's top story.

sources.title: Learn anything with the /teach skill
sources.note: Stateful teaching skill with adaptive lessons

radar.text: Firecrawl beat Claude's native web fetch on 4 of 5 e-commerce sites — Ben AI now defaults to it (and to CLIs over MCPs for tokens).
Claude + Gmail triaging a whole inbox into a prioritized sheet with send-ready drafts — Dan Martell's daily driver.
Supercomputer shipped a product, brand kit, and video ads from one prompt — market-gap analysis before code generation.
'Cursor just crushed Claude Code' — no transcript available yet; watch the demo before believing the headline.
